title: 10 best herbal decoctions for upset stomach
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for upset stomach are a natural remedy that can provide relief from digestive discomfort, nausea, and bloating. These decoctions are made by steeping herbs in hot water to release their active compounds, which then help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. Examples of herbal decoctions that can aid in alleviating upset stomach include peppermint tea, ginger infusion, and chamomile broth. By sipping on these soothing concoctions, individuals can find relief from symptoms and improve their overall quality of life, allowing them to enjoy daily activities without discomfort or distraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its natural ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion. The herb's bitter properties help to increase saliva production, which aids in breaking down food more efficiently. Additionally, yellow gentian decoctions can help to soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by upset stomachs. As a result, this herbal remedy can provide fast relief from bloating, cramps, and nausea associated with indigestion and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_properties: Bitter principles: Yellow gentian contains bitter compounds that stimulate digestive secretions, increasing the flow of bile and digestive enzymes to relieve indigestion and alleviate bloating.
Alkaloids: The alkaloid content in yellow gentian has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract and alleviating symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and cramps.

Tannins: Tannins present in yellow gentian decoctions have astringent properties, which can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their bitter compounds, roots are effective in stimulating digestive enzymes and relieving symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest gentiana lutea roots in autumn or early spring after drying them to preserve their medicinal properties.
Measure out 30-40 grams of dried gentiana lutea root and rinse it with cold water to remove impurities.
Combine the rinsed root with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container to create a decoction.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15 minutes before straining it through a fine-mesh sieve into another container to separate the liquid from the solids.
Consume the resulting gentiana lutea decoction immediately, taking 100 milliliters as needed up to three times daily to soothe stomach upset.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with upset stomach because it contains compounds that soothe and calm the digestive system. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines. Additionally, licorice flavonoids have been shown to stimulate the production of mucin, a thick protective lining in the gut that helps to prevent damage from digestive acids and enzymes. This results in a calming effect on the stomach, reducing symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and heartburn associated with upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Flavonoids: Licorice contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, leading to improved digestion and reduced discomfort.

Licoricidin: This compound has anti-spasmodic properties, which helps to relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing cramps, spasms, and other symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used to create a soothing and anti-inflammatory decoction that can help alleviate symptoms of upset stomach, including bloating and discomfort.

Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes are rich in glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal decoctions for calming upset stomachs.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and add it to a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat two cups of water in a kettle or on the stovetop until it reaches a boil.
Pour the boiling water over the glycyrrhiza glabra root in the cup or teapot, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction into a clean cup using a tea strainer or piece of cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and drink immediately, or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with upset stomach because it stimulates digestion, reducing inflammation and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger also help to soothe the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as bloating and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's natural antimicrobial effects can help to eliminate harmful bacteria and other microorganisms that may be contributing to an upset stomach. By promoting a healthy gut environment, herbal ginger decoctions can provide fast and effective relief for digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Inhibition: Ginger's active compound, gingerol, inhibits the production of digestive enzymes that break down food too quickly, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with upset stomach.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Activity: Ginger's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as cramping, bloating, and discomfort.

Gastric Motility Regulation: Ginger's bioactive compounds, particularly gingerol and shogaol, regulate gastric motility by increasing the movement of food through the digestive system, reducing symptoms like constipation and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant are used to make medicinal decoctions because they contain natural compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive system.
Roots: The roots of the ginger plant are used to make medicinal decoctions as they contain active compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots of 2-3 inches in length and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Cut the ginger roots into thin slices and grind them into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for about 5 minutes.
Combine 10-15 grams of ground ginger with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with upset stomach because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The gel of the aloe leaf contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to relax the muscles in the gut, alleviate cramps, and ease spasms. Additionally, aloe decoctions can help restore the natural balance of gut flora, promoting a healthy digestive environment and alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating, nausea, and diarrhea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach, including cramps, bloating, and diarrhea.

Soothing and protective mucilage: The gel-like substance found in aloe vera contains mucilages that form a soothing film on the gut lining, protecting it from irritation and damage caused by acid reflux, digestive enzymes, or other irritants, which can help calm an upset stomach.

Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants, such as vitamins C and E, help neutralize free radicals in the body that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract, further exacerbating an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including upset stomach, due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Aloe leaves contain anthraquinone compounds that help alleviate symptoms of upset stomach, such as pain and inflammation, by reducing spasms in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-20 grams of fresh or dried aloe vera leaves and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and steep them in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature before storing it in an airtight container.
Consume 1/2 cup or 100-150 milliliters of the cooled decoction 3 times daily for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's mucilages help to calm irritated linings in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, fenugreek's fiber content can help to regulate bowel movements and prevent diarrhea or constipation. As a result, drinking fenugreek decoctions can provide relief from symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and stomach cramps, allowing for a smoother digestion process and overall sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fenugreek contains anti-inflammatory compounds like galactomannans, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating and cramps.

Mucilage content: Fenugreek seeds contain a type of mucilage called galactose-rich polysaccharides, which forms a protective barrier in the stomach, soothing irritations, and reducing inflammation, making it an effective natural anti-inflammatory agent for stomach upset.

Antioxidant properties: Fenugreek's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage the gut lining and cause digestive issues, promoting overall gut health and reducing symptoms of upset stomach such as nausea and diarrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fenugreek roots have a soothing effect on the digestive system, helping to calm irritation and inflammation in the stomach.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds contain mucilage, a natural gel-like substance that can help soothe and protect the stomach lining.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried fenugreek seeds and bring them to room temperature.
Combine the dried fenugreek seeds with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Store the fenugreek decoction in the refrigerator for up to three days before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with upset stomach because its soothing properties calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms. The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, gently ease cramping and discomfort, while its antibacterial properties help eliminate any underlying infections that may be contributing to the issue. As a natural relaxant, chamomile also reduces stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate stomach upset, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation that benefits digestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach like bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic compounds in chamomile, including flavonoids and terpenes, relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can exacerbate upset stomach symptoms.

Soothing mucous membrane property: Chamomile's soothing compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation of the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, which can help calm symptoms like heartburn, nausea, and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and other flavonoids, chamomile roots help soothe and calm digestive issues, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain a high concentration of bisabolol, an anti-inflammatory compound that reduces symptoms of upset stomach, such as bloating and cramping.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a clean glass jar.
Add one cup of boiling water to the jar and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another clean glass container.
Discard the solids and use the liquid as is or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Drink one to two cups of the decoction after meals or as needed to help soothe upset stomach.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its soothing and calming properties. The volatile oils present in lemon balm, such as citral and geraniol, have a gentle relaxing effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and inflammation that can cause discomfort and pain. Additionally, lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to ease cramps and contractions in the stomach, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. As a result, lemon balm decoctions can provide relief from symptoms such as bloating, indigestion, and nausea associated with upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm decoctions contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating and cramping.

Antispasmodic effects: The volatile oils present in lemon balm decoctions have antispasmodic properties that can help relax the smooth muscles of the stomach, reducing spasms and cramps associated with indigestion and upset stomach.

Carminative properties: Lemon balm decoctions contain compounds that can help eliminate gas and bloating from the digestive system by stimulating digestion and improving intestinal motility, which can provide relief from symptoms such as discomfort and flatulence.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have a calming effect on the digestive system, making them effective in soothing upset stomach and relieving digestive discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease digestive spasms, providing relief from stomach upset and cramping.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves measuring one teaspoon per cup water.
Combine the herb with cold- filtered water in a non-reactive pot bringing it to boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes allowing the flavors to meld together slowly.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container immediately.
Discard solids and store cooled decoction in refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley decoctions helps with upset stomach because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, volatile oils, and other bioactive compounds that work together to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of parsley help reduce inflammation in the gut, while its carminative effects relieve gas and bloating. Additionally, parsley's ability to stimulate digestion and improve bowel function helps to restore balance to the digestive system, providing rapid relief from symptoms such as cramps, diarrhea, and nausea associated with upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Carminative and antispasmodic properties: Parsley helps to reduce muscle spasms in the digestive tract, relieving cramps and bloating associated with upset stomachs.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Parsley's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds soothe irritated tissues and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can exacerbate stomach issues.

remedy_parts: Roots: Parsley roots have been traditionally used to treat indigestion and other gastrointestinal issues due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.
Seeds: Parsley seeds have been known to reduce spasms in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach and promoting digestive regularity.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried petroselinum crispum leaves into a clean glass cup.
Heat 250 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan over medium heat until boiling occurs.
Add the measured petroselinum crispum leaves to the boiling water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for an additional 5 minutes before straining.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass cup for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its soothing properties, which calm inflammation in the digestive tract. The decoction's caraway seeds contain a unique compound called cuminaldehyde, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. This combination helps to ease symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with an upset stomach. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles, herbal caraway decoctions promote a sense of relief and aid in the recovery from digestive distress.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Caraway decoction helps relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause abdominal pain and discomfort.

Carminative property: Caraway's volatile oils, such as limonene and beta-pinene, help to release gas bubbles in the stomach and intestines, alleviating bloating, flatulence, and discomfort associated with indigestion.

Antimicrobial property: Caraway's antimicrobial compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, such as Helicobacter pylori, which can contribute to gastritis, ulcers, and other digestive disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Caraway roots have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues like upset stomach.
Seeds: Caraway seeds have a natural carminative effect, helping to expel gas and reduce bloating caused by indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality carum carvi seeds and dried mint leaves in equal proportions weighing about 20 grams.
Combine the ingredients with four cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let it steep for 15-20 minutes allowing the flavors to meld together.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in an airtight container refrigerated for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its soothing properties that calm digestive issues. The roots of the burdock plant contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that feeds good bacteria in the gut, promoting a balanced gut microbiome. This leads to reduced inflammation and improved digestion, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Additionally, burdock's anti-inflammatory compounds help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinones: Burdock root contains anthraquinones, which have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to soothe and reduce inflammation in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as cramps and diarrhea.

Inulin: Inulin, a type of fiber found in burdock root, helps to regulate bowel movements, prevent constipation, and support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can help to restore balance to an upset stomach.

Phenolic acids: Burdock root is rich in phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms of upset stomach like bloating and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and mucilage, burdock roots soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
Fruits: The seeds of the burdock fruit are high in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried arctium lapa root, 8 ounces of water, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried root with the water in the glass jar and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding solids.
Bring the liquid to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 5-7 minutes before serving.
Store any leftover decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with upset stomach because they contain bioactive compounds that possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The roots and leaves of the dandelion plant have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, including bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Inulin: Inulin, a type of fiber found in dandelion root, acts as a prebiotic, promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, regulating bowel movements, and soothing stomach irritation.

Buds: The buds of the dandelion plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help soothe digestive issues such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale root material or two teaspoons of fresh material.
Combine the herb material with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid using a piece of cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Take one-half to one cup of the decoction three times daily after meals and at bedtime.
Store any leftover decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to two days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its soothing properties, which calm digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. The natural compounds present in grapes, like flavonoids and polyphenols, have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce irritation in the digestive tract, allowing the gut to heal and recover more quickly. Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve stomach cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.

Barks: Containing tannins and other compounds, grape barks have natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help stop diarrhea and alleviate stomach upset.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vitis vinifera leaves and stems in early morning.
Rinse the herbs with filtered water to remove dirt and debris then chop.
Measure one teaspoon of chopped herb for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the herb mixture in boiling water for 10-15 minutes then strain.
Store the cooled decoction in airtight containers for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The active compound curcumin, found in high concentrations in turmeric roots, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that reduce inflammation and irritation in the gut, easing symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Digestive enzyme stimulant properties: Turmeric has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, such as amylase and lipase, which can help break down food properly and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and gas.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm digestive issues, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal decoctions for upset stomach.
Roots: Known for their ability to reduce inflammation and ease symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), turmeric roots are a popular choice for herbal remedies targeting digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried berberis aristata root powder into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Add one cup of boiling water to the cup or teapot and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup or container.
Discard the solids and add one tablespoon of honey to the decoction if desired for taste.
Drink the entire decoction immediately after preparation, ideally within 15-20 minutes for maximum benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle decoctions helps with upset stomach because it has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues such as indigestion, nausea, and bloating. Seeds: Milk thistle seeds are rich in oil and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm digestive issues such as upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried silybum marianum seeds per eight ounces of water.
Bring the water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan.
Add the measured silybum marianum seeds and reduce heat to a simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before consuming within 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried plantago major leaves and flowers measure one ounce.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine with four cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes duration.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rumex crispus leaves and stems of 30-40 cm length for 500g quantity.
Rinse the harvested material with clean water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Chop the rumex crispus into smaller pieces and dry them in a warm place or oven at 60°c for 1 hour.
Transfer 100g of dried and chopped rumex crispus to a pot with 2 liters of boiling water and simmer for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with upset stomach because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. Additionally, peppermint's antispasmodic properties can help ease irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) symptoms such as diarrhea or constipation, promoting regular bowel movements and overall digestive balance.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), gastritis, and other stomach disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant have a calming effect on the digestive system, making them effective in relieving symptoms of upset stomach such as nausea and cramps.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint herb per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in a glass container at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Consume 1 cup of the cooled decoction as needed for stomach upset relief.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemongrass leaves or dried lemongrass powder in quantities of one tablespoon per cup of water.

Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Store the herbal decoction in the refrigerator for up to three days and consume as needed by stirring one tablespoon of the cooled decoction into one cup of warm water.
